file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      app.kubernetes.io/component: head
      bench.ray.cluster: ray
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        app.kubernetes.io/component: head
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: ray-head
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        env:
          - name: MY_POD_IP
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: status.podIP
          - name: RAY_NODE_ID
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.uid
          - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
            value: "1"
        command:
          - sh
          - -c
          - |
            ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
        ports:
          - containerPort: 6379
            name: gcs

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head-service
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
  ports:
    - name: gcs
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379

file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ray-worker
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
      bench.ray.cluster: ray
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: ray-worker
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        env:
          - name: MY_POD_IP
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: status.podIP
          - name: RAY_NODE_ID
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.uid
          - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
            value: "1"
        command:
          - sh
          - -c
          - |
            ray start --address=ray-head:6379 --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --num-cpus=1 --block

content.thinking:
The error `Can't find a node_ip_address.json file` suggests the ray-client is trying to connect but can't find a local session file. This suggests the ray-client pod itself needs a local `ray start` or `ray.init` session first, or the connection is being treated as a local mode instead of a cluster connection.

Wait, the error says it's connecting to `ray-head:6379` (I can see `Connecting to existing Ray cluster at address: ray-head:6379...`) but then it's looking for a `node_ip_address.json` file locally. This is because in older versions of Ray, when you do `ray.init(address='...')`, it starts a local raylet on the client side too. 
The client pod might be running an older Ray version than the 2.9.0 on the cluster, which could explain why it's trying to initialize a local session. I should check what version is actually installed on the client pod.

content.text: The client is getting a version mismatch or local session issue. Let me check the ray-client's Ray version.

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
input.description: Check ray version on client pod

input.command: kubectl -n ray get pod ray-client -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].image}'
